stem: An orchard has 7 rows of pear trees and 4 rows of apple trees. Each row of pear trees has 10 trees 25 feet or taller and 5 trees shorter than 25 feet. Each row of apple trees has 12 trees 25 feet or taller and 8 trees shorter than 25 feet. A tree from one of these rows will be selected at random. What is the probability of selecting an apple tree, given that the tree is 25 feet or taller?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. There are 7 × 10 = 70 pear trees and 4 × 12 = 48 apple trees that are 25 feet or taller, for a total of 118. So P(apple | ≥ 25 ft) = 48/118 = 24/59.

Choice A counts rows incorrectly. Choice B uses one row only. Choice C swaps numerator with pear count.
